More specifically, Gymnorebutia neocumingii is classified within the subfamily Rebutioideae. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and relationships to other cacti. The exact classification of Gymnorebutia neocumingii is still a subject of ongoing research and study.

intro: Gymnorea neocumingii, also known as Gymnema sylvestre, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The leaves of Gymnorea neocumingii have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including aiding in weight loss, regulating blood sugar levels, and improving lipid profiles. The bioactive constituents of the plant include gymnemic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties, as well as other flavonoids and terpenoids. Gymnorea neocumingii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as supplements, teas, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs to support weight loss and blood sugar regulation.
